CONCAT.description: Concatenates two or more string arguments into a single string. Returns NULL if any argument is NULL. For NULL-safe concatenation, use CONCAT_WS or IFNULL wrappers.

CONCAT_WS.description: Concatenates strings with a separator between each pair, automatically skipping NULL values. The first argument is the delimiter used between the remaining arguments.

SUBSTRING.description: Extracts a substring from the given string starting at position pos (1-based) for len characters. Negative pos counts from the end. Also aliased as SUBSTR and MID.

REPLACE.description: Returns a string with all occurrences of from_str replaced by to_str in the original string. The search is case-sensitive and does not support regular expressions.

LENGTH.description: Returns the length of the string in bytes. For multi-byte character sets use CHAR_LENGTH instead, which returns the number of characters regardless of encoding.

NOW.description: Returns the current date and time as a DATETIME value in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format. The value is determined at statement execution start and remains constant within the statement.

CURDATE.description: Returns the current date as a DATE value in YYYY-MM-DD format without the time component. Equivalent to CURRENT_DATE and useful for date-only comparisons and filters.

DATE_FORMAT.description: Formats a date value according to the specified format string using specifiers like %Y for 4-digit year, %m for month, %d for day, %H for hour, %i for minute, %s for second.

DATEDIFF.description: Returns the number of days between two date or datetime expressions, calculated as date1 minus date2. Only the date parts are used in the calculation, ignoring time components.

DATE_ADD.description: Adds a time interval to a date or datetime value. Units include SECOND, MINUTE, HOUR, DAY, WEEK, MONTH, QUARTER, YEAR, and compound units like DAY_HOUR.

IF.description: Evaluates a boolean condition and returns the second argument if true or the third argument if false. A concise inline conditional expression for SELECT lists and computed columns.

IFNULL.description: Returns expr1 if it is not NULL, otherwise returns expr2. A concise NULL-handling function commonly used to provide default values for potentially NULL columns or expressions.

COALESCE.description: Returns the first non-NULL value in the argument list, or NULL if all arguments are NULL. Standard SQL function for cascading fallback values across multiple nullable expressions.

CASE.signature: CASE expr WHEN val THEN result ELSE default END -> ANY
CASE.description: Provides conditional logic in SQL expressions with two forms: simple comparing an expression to values, and searched evaluating boolean conditions. Returns the matching or ELSE result.

COUNT.description: Aggregate function that returns the number of non-NULL values in the expression. COUNT(*) counts all rows including NULLs. COUNT(DISTINCT expr) counts unique non-NULL values.

SUM.description: Aggregate function that returns the sum of all non-NULL values in the expression. Returns NULL if there are no matching rows. Works with numeric types and implicit conversion.

AVG.description: Aggregate function that returns the arithmetic mean of non-NULL values in the expression. NULL values are excluded from both the sum and count used in the average calculation.

GROUP_CONCAT.signature: GROUP_CONCAT([DISTINCT] expr [ORDER BY col] [SEPARATOR str]) -> VARCHAR
GROUP_CONCAT.description: Aggregate function that concatenates values from multiple rows into a single string, with optional DISTINCT filtering, ORDER BY sorting, and custom SEPARATOR (default comma).

JSON_EXTRACT.description: Extracts data from a JSON document at the specified JSONPath expression. Returns NULL if the path does not exist. The ->> operator unquotes the result to a string.

JSON_OBJECT.description: Creates a JSON object from the specified key-value pairs. Keys must be strings and are automatically quoted. Values are converted to appropriate JSON types.

JSON_ARRAYAGG.description: Aggregate function that collects values from multiple rows into a JSON array. Preserves NULL values as JSON null elements within the resulting array output.

CAST.description: Converts an expression to the specified data type. Supported target types include CHAR, DATE, DATETIME, DECIMAL, SIGNED, UNSIGNED, BINARY, JSON, and spatial types.

ROW_NUMBER.signature: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY col ORDER BY col) -> BIGINT
ROW_NUMBER.description: Window function that assigns a unique sequential integer to each row within its partition, ordered by the specified columns. Available in MySQL 8.0+ for ranking and pagination.

RANK.signature: RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY col ORDER BY col) -> BIGINT
RANK.description: Window function that assigns a rank to each row within its partition with gaps for ties. Equal values receive the same rank, and the next rank skips accordingly.
